title: eupatorium rugosum f. verticillatum
intro: Eupatorium rugosum f. verticillatum, also known as Eastern Joe Pye Weed, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium rugosum f. verticillatum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to treat a range of ailments. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and health benefits of this plant.
